header.title: Voices of Innovation
header.subtitle: Browse the community builders, technologists, and storytellers sharing their experience on stage at WTM Montreal 2026.

list.name: Mélisane Lafrenaye
list.bio: Mélisane Lafrenaye is a developer and technical team lead at the video game studio Triple Boris. After starting her career in the military sector then in biomedical robotics, she has dedicated herself to the video game industry for five years. She specializes mainly in frontend development with, among others, the Unity game engine. Invested and passionate about knowledge sharing, she now actively contributes to the evolution and maintenance of best practices within the studio.

list.name: Melanie Guerin
list.bio: Mélanie Guérin is a principal partner at FJNR, where she accompanies organizations and entrepreneurs in the design and realization of digital projects, with a pragmatic approach focused on value and decision-making clarity. She has led many projects involving multidisciplinary teams, in development as well as product strategy. She is also a partner at EmoScienS, an AI startup specialized in emotional recognition. Committed to the Montreal ecosystem, she is the president of Montréal-Python, actively contributing to knowledge sharing and community development.

list.name: Rashida Geddes
list.bio: Rashida is a talent acquisition leader at Bell, focused on identifying and nurturing diverse talent in the telecommunications and tech sectors.

list.name: Maya Toussaint
list.bio: Maya Toussaint is a DEI Consultant and speaker, specializing in inclusive hiring practices and building equitable workplace cultures.

list.name: Annalise Iten
list.bio: Annalise is an Employment Specialist at YES Montreal, helping people navigate their career paths and overcome barriers to employment in the Montreal area.

list.name: Ulrich Aïvodji
list.bio: Ulrich is an assistant professor in computer science at ÉTS in the department of software engineering and information technology. His research areas are Responsible AI, privacy protection and cybersecurity. His current research work focuses on several Responsible AI issues, such as privacy protection, explainability and algorithmic fairness.

list.name: Khaoula Chehbouni
list.bio: Khaoula Chehbouni is a third-year PhD student at McGill University and Mila (Quebec AI Institute). She received the prestigious FRQNT doctoral training scholarship to conduct research on fairness and security in large language models. Previously, she worked as a senior data scientist at Statistics Canada. She holds a Master's degree in business intelligence from HEC Montréal, for which she received the Best Thesis Award. Her research interests include responsible AI, fairness and security issues in large language models.

list.name: Afaf Taïk
list.bio: Dr. Afaf Taïk is an assistant professor at the Université de Sherbrooke and currently a postdoctoral researcher at Mila – Quebec AI Institute and the Université de Montréal, working on the intersection of fairness and privacy in machine learning. She obtained a PhD in electrical engineering in 2022 from the Université de Sherbrooke, where she worked on problems related to distributed machine learning algorithms. Afaf has received numerous awards and recognizations for her work, such as the Claire DesChênes (2023) and FRQNT (2024) postdoctoral scholarships.

list.name: Noémi Mercier
list.bio: Noémi Mercier is a journalist and host. With a particular interest in social and political issues, she has led several documentaries and series as a host, content producer or director, including 'IA: l'angle mort'. Noémi is also recognized for her many significant reports published in L’actualité and Québec Science. She has received four Judith-Jasmin prizes and seven gold medals at the Canadian Magazine Awards, as well as nominations for the Michener, Albert-London and Gémeaux Awards. She holds a BA in psychology from McGill University.

list.name: Anne Nguyen
list.bio: Principal Director responsible for artificial intelligence at the Quebec Innovation Council, Anne Nguyen works to make AI a lever for responsible innovation, aligned with the values of inclusion, equality and humanity that guide her career. Before joining the CIQ, she worked for 18 years in the health network, where she led several strategic projects in digital transformation and data valuation. Anne holds a degree in Business Analytics from Harvard Business School, a Product Management Master certification from the Pragmatic Institute, a Lean Six Sigma Black Belt from McGill University and a management certificate from the Université de Montréal.

list.name: Marie-Gabrielle Ménard
list.bio: Marie-Gabrielle Ménard, Liberal MP for Hochelaga—Rosemont-East, Parliamentary Secretary to the Minister for Women and Gender Equality and Secretary of State (Small Business and Tourism). Before making the leap into politics, Ms. Ménard made a name for herself as an artist, reporter and host, notably on radio and television. An experienced communicator, woman of heart and action, she is committed with conviction to carrying the voice of the population. We will have the privilege of hearing her perspective on the place of women in work environments that are still predominantly male.

list.name: Alexandra Nemery
list.bio:
Alexandra Nemery is a professor and researcher in user experience (UX) design at the École de technologie supérieure (ÉTS) in Montreal. With a PhD in ergonomics, she specializes in persuasive design, gamification, and user experience evaluation in complex contexts such as serious games, virtual reality, and interactive scientific tools. 
 With extensive professional experience in the video game industry (Ubisoft, Square Enix, Haven Studio), she now conducts research projects at the interface of science, technology, and design, particularly focusing on content gamification, explainable AI, and ethical and inclusive design.

list.name: Angie Ariza
list.bio: Angie Ariza is the AI Lead Developer at Plus Company, where she leads the Smart Persona project and specializes in scaling AI systems from proof-of-concept to production. Her expertise spans Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG), AI agents, distributed systems, and generative models, with a strong track record of turning cutting-edge research into production-ready solutions. She has led impactful projects across fraud detection, forecasting, and large-scale data platforms. Beyond her technical leadership, Angie is a Women Techmakers Ambassador, committed to advancing the visibility and inclusion of women in technology.

list.name: Chloé Freslon
list.bio: Chloé Freslon is the founder of URelles, a company offering innovative solutions to support organizations aspiring to a more inclusive and equitable culture.

list.name: Claude Cardinal
list.bio:
A doctoral candidate in computer science (video game project management) at UQAC, Claude Cardinal has a diverse background in computer science, education, and public administration. He contributed to skills development at Eidos Montreal. 
 With over thirty years of experience in education and instructional design, he previously directed the CeFTI at the University of Sherbrooke and worked as a programmer-analyst, application architect, and project manager in various technological environments.

list.name: JL Marechaux
list.bio:
JL Marechaux is a Principal AI Consultant at ServiceNow. He brings extensive experience in applied artificial intelligence and digital transformation.​  
 During his career, JL has held a diverse range of roles, including AI technical advisor, Cloud specialist, head of technology and principal enterprise architect in tech companies like Google, Ivado Labs, Blue Yonder and IBM. He was also a course lecturer at McGill University.​  
 In his last role prior to joining ServiceNow, JL was part of the 'Solutions & Thought Leadership' team at Google, helping North American companies use artificial intelligence to accelerate their digital transformation. He worked on advanced analytics and applied machine learning (ML) solutions for clients across key sectors such as retail, finance, travel, automotive, and insurance.​  
 JL actively contributes to the Montreal technology community as a mentor, a public speaker, an ambassador for Women in Data Science (WiDS) and co-organizer of the AI Tinkerers Montréal group.

list.name: Ludociels (Thomas & Stéphanie)
list.bio:
Thomas Gaudy is a UX designer, computer science researcher, and co-founder of the social economy organization Ludociels pour tous (Games for All), which works to promote the inclusion and accessibility of digital interfaces. He holds a PhD in computer science and specializes in the study of digital ergonomics, cognitive and social psychology, and experimental games, leading him to design accessible video games, particularly for audiences with various disabilities. At Ludociels pour tous, Thomas works as a UX design consultant, offering ergonomic design services and guiding socially-oriented digital projects to ensure their inclusivity. His professional commitment thus combines research, teaching, UX consulting, and the creation of inclusive digital entertainment, reflecting his passion for the social impact of digital technologies. 
 Stéphanie Akré is a social entrepreneur and co-founder of Ludociels pour tous, a social economy organization that helps design inclusive digital tools. Before co-founding Ludociels pour tous, Stéphanie worked as a massage therapist/physiotherapist. It was this experience in providing care and support that led her to reflect on the challenges of accessibility and inclusion in daily life, particularly through digital technology. Her professional journey has been marked by a significant transition: after experiencing burnout, she chose to redirect her career towards social entrepreneurship and learn on the job the skills related to managing an organization, digital communication, and collective entrepreneurship.

list.name: Mahsa Rezaei
list.bio:
Mahsa is a Future of Work Architect and Social Entrepreneur with over 15 years of experience, evolving from Tech to leader in building career resilience for women in the AI economy. 
 As founder of 'MamaNest', she has pioneered two flagship events 'MamaTech AI Studio', and the 'Women's Career Conference' alongside other activities to build a comprehensive career-growth ecosystem for women. Starting with Moms, Mahsa founded the 'Intelligent Nest' next to deliver AI-enhanced employment services to English-speaking women in Quebec's Professional, Scientific, and Technical Services sector. 
 Beyond her own ventures,She is deeply committed to the broader landscape of women's empowerment and Social Impact and has contributed to impactful initiatives including WEDO Canada, the Social Business Creation competition from HEC Montreal, and the N.E.W Venture Program. She combines technical expertise with heart-led strategy to prove that career resilience and caregiving are not mutually exclusive.

list.name: Mahzad Kalantari
list.bio: Mahzad Kalantari is a machine learning and data science expert with a PhD in computer science and computer vision and over 20 years of experience in applied machine learning and data science. She works in the video game industry, where her focus is on integrating reinforcement learning into games to build intelligent systems, support game development, and enhance player experiences. She also applies machine learning techniques to automated game testing, helping teams improve quality, scalability, and production efficiency. She currently works at Wizards of the Coast, where she focuses on integrating reinforcement learning–based automated testing across video game studios.

list.name: Mariam Jabara
list.bio: Mariam has been in the AI space for the last 5 years. Currently a Senior Field Engineer for Arcee AI, she has previous experience at Google Research and Deloitte. Her values are rooted in building community and using AI responsibly.

list.name: Michell Payano
list.bio:
Michell Payano started her career as an Economist in the public sector in Dominican Republic, where she developed a foundation in data driven policymaking. Recognizing the transformative potential of AI to address complex societal challenges, she made a transition to technology by pursuing a Master's degree in Computer Science with a specialization in machine learning at Université de Montréal, in collaboration with Mila. Her professional trajectory reflects an interdisciplinary vision, spanning from economic policy analysis to cutting edge research in computer vision and she is currently conducting research in multi-agent reinforcement learning for policy making. 
 Beyond her professional and academic background, Michell is a passionate advocate for democratizing access to technology. She has served as an adjunct professor in her home country, and in Canada, she served as a mentor to young girls through Technovation Montréal, working to close the gender gap in tech by inspiring and equipping the next generation of women in AI.

list.name: Nadia Seraiocco PhD
list.bio: Nadia Seraiocco, PhD in Communication, jointly holds degrees from Concordia University, the University of Montreal, and UQAM. After a career in communications from 1999 to 2014, working as a journalist and for organizations such as the National Gallery of Canada, VIA Rail, and the City of Montreal, her curiosity about technology led her to develop expertise in digital communications. Since 2013, she has taught digital media at UQAM's School of Media. Her research interests include creative research and the philosophical and ethical questions surrounding the use of individuals' personal data. For her doctoral thesis, defended in 2023 and entitled 'The Digital Double and Identity: Between Reflection, Amplification, and Diffraction,' she created a chatbot using her personal data that interacted on Twitter. Her master's thesis focused on Fluxus films, and she also pursued graduate studies at HEC Montréal. A blogger since 2002, she has been a columnist at ICI Première and regularly appears in the media.

list.name: Nathalie Sanon
list.bio: Nathalie Sanon is a manager specializing in AI training and its social impacts.

list.name: Nogol Madani
list.bio: Nogol Madani is a future-focused workplace expert, speaker, and facilitator. She is the founder and CEO of a company dedicated to helping leaders build high-performing, inclusive, future-ready teams through psychological safety. Nogol started her career as an engineer, and as a first-generation immigrant and female professional, she experienced firsthand in the workplace how many leaders weren’t unlocking potential, they were stifling it. This realization sparked her mission to help develop workplaces where people thrive. Nogol has earned the trust of organizations like Google, SAP, Rio Tinto, BeaverTails, C2 Montreal, and Desjardins, guiding them to collaborate, communicate, and create better together. She is also a faculty member at the McGill Executive Institute, where she shares her expertise on leadership and team dynamics.

list.name: Stacy Véronneau
list.bio:
I wear many hats, but my primary role is to architect cloud solutions with a focus on security and infrastructure. In this capacity, I've honed my competencies by defining technical pre-sales processes and engaging with customers to tailor cloud offerings that address their unique challenges and drive growth.

My approach is that of a trusted advisor, leveraging extensive experience in cloud services to build and develop offerings that not only meet client needs but also empower delivery teams to execute successfully. With fluency in English and French, I thrive in collaborative environments, inspiring and sharing insights with both colleagues and clients in the realm of cloud integration and innovation.

list.name: Vivienne Suen
list.bio: Vivienne Suen est architecte en cybersécurité et possède un talent certain pour la communication. Forte de plus de 15 ans d'expérience, elle conseille les organisations informatiques en matière de solutions et de stratégie. À la Banque TD, elle dirige l'équipe d'architecture de cybersécurité, où elle conçoit les contrôles de cybersécurité de la banque, oriente la stratégie technologique et définit la vision architecturale pour assurer notre avenir. Conférencière recherchée lors de nombreux congrès et groupes d'utilisateurs, elle excelle dans la vulgarisation des concepts complexes de la cybersécurité et la conception de solutions pratiques répondant aux besoins et aux normes de sécurité. Vivienne vit à NDG avec son mari, son fils et un Vallhund suédois très énergique.
